vehicle.label: Long-Range Surveillance Wing
vehicle.desc: Fixed-wing flying-wing UAV, 2m wingspan, 4+ hour endurance

parts.label: Center Body Pod
parts.spec: Flying-wing center pod, equipment bay door, wing-spar reinforcement, motor pylon at rear

parts.label: Wing Spar (2-piece)
parts.spec: 20mm CFRP main spar, 2-piece centerjoint, anti-rotation key, 800mm half-span each

parts.label: Wing Rib Set x 12
parts.spec: Wing rib NACA airfoil profile, lightening pockets, leading edge sock, trailing edge taper

parts.label: Elevon Servo Pocket x 2
parts.spec: Elevon servo pocket, MG90S or DS215MG, control horn linkage, removable access cover

parts.label: Motor + Pusher Prop Mount
parts.spec: Rear pusher 2814 BLDC mount, 10x7 folding prop clearance, thrust line +1deg down

parts.label: GPS + Pitot Boom
parts.spec: Nose boom 8mm, GPS at tip, pitot static port forward, BEC airflow shielded

parts.label: Camera Gimbal Bay
parts.spec: Forward downward camera gimbal bay 70mm deep, 2-axis micro gimbal, optical window

parts.label: Battery Bay (Slide)
parts.spec: 4S 16000mAh Li-ion slide-in bay, CG adjust slot, top access hatch, balance lead window

parts.label: Integration Service Backbone
parts.spec: Wing/fuselage integration service backbone with assembly_datum pads, harness_raceway, service_hatch, connector_keying, anti-rotation wing datum, and visible load-path inspection marks

requirements: Provide structural support for wing spars
Include an equipment bay door for maintenance access
Mount a motor pylon for a pusher propeller
Ensure cooling air flow from motor pod inlet to equipment bay
Maintain integrity under operational loads
Allow for electronic integration within the pod
Optimize cooling efficiency
Minimize unnecessary weight while maintaining strength

generated_parts.label: Wing Spar (2-piece)
generated_parts.spec: 20mm CFRP main spar, 2-piece centerjoint, anti-rotation key, 800mm half-span each

requirements: Structures subsystem: Main spar, ribs, carry-through, fuselage frames; continuous load path
Critical interfaces: spar_socket, anti_rotation_key, rib, carry_through, frame
Datum visibility: Primary locating faces, rail guides, axis rings, spar sockets, or mating pads are visible and labeled
Clearance named: Critical slip, snap, pin, hinge, and slide interfaces state plausible clearances
Interface count: Each subsystem has at least three adjacent interfaces or datums
Standard fit vocabulary: Fits are named with standard intent: clearance fit for sliding, transition for locating, interference/press only where justified, with a numeric allowance
Standard hardware dimensioning: Holes, slots, and pockets match real catalog hardware sizes (M3/M4 clearance holes, standard bearing OD, common pin diameters)
Spar datum access: Spar socket, anti-rotation keys, and center datum remain visible and inspectable
Mission payload non-weapon boundary: Payload bay is labeled non-weapon/display-only with removable mission/sensor module
CG battery service: Battery bay uses slide rail, CG marks, and independent payload/battery removal
Load path continuity: Lift, landing, and payload loads trace through continuous structure: spar to carry-through to fuselage frames, with fittings at every load transfer

verify.rule: Each required evidence feature must appear in part_tree/geometry_ops
cad_brief.mass_est_g must be a numeric grams value
Each physical feature must include args.at [x,y,z] and real dimensions
Name exact neighboring subsystem labels when they mate
Critical joints need visible insert/washer/locking/preload evidence

risk.desc: Potential misalignment during assembly due to complex interface
risk.mit: Implement visual alignment aids and pre-assembly checks

risk.desc: Risk of overloading the spar if load path is not properly traced
risk.mit: Conduct thorough structural analysis and stress testing

risk.desc: Inadequate clearance for moving parts may cause jamming
risk.mit: Ensure all critical interfaces have appropriate clearances

risk.desc: Incorrect hardware dimensioning could lead to improper fit
risk.mit: Use standard hardware sizes and ensure proper fit classes

risk.desc: Hidden spar alignment evidence may complicate maintenance
risk.mit: Maintain visibility of spar alignment features throughout assembly

_subsystem_plan.subsystem_role: Servo pockets, control horns, pushrod/linkage channels
physics_paths: Load path: Servo loads transferred to wing structure via servo pocket and control horn
Flow path: Control signals from flight controller to servo via connector_mate
Thermal path: Heat dissipation from servo to surrounding air via convection
Motion path: Control horn movement translates servo rotation to elevon deflection
Pressure path: Aerodynamic pressure on elevon transmitted to wing structure
integration_opportunities: Optimize servo pocket shape for better load distribution
Improve control horn design for smoother motion
Enhance pushrod channel routing for reduced friction
verification_focus: Mass verification: Ensure total mass does not exceed 150g
Load testing: Verify servo loads do not exceed structural limits
Thermal testing: Confirm heat dissipation meets requirements
